topics.title: Ask for clarity (script)
topics.subtitle: A short script to get what you need without drama.

bridge_messages.INDIVIDUAL: Be specific: what decision, what by when, and what you tried.
bridge_messages.PEOPLE_LEADER: Reduce uncertainty: what changes, what stays, where to ask, and what to do next.

role_specific_framing.INDIVIDUAL: Use this when you feel stuck because you don't know what's expected.
role_specific_framing.PEOPLE_LEADER: Use this when your team is waiting because decision rights are unclear.

INDIVIDUAL: Ask what 'good' looks like this week.

INDIVIDUAL.label: One-sentence ask
INDIVIDUAL.text: Here's what I need to move forward: [X]. Who owns this decision, and what's the next step?

PEOPLE_LEADER.label: Manager reply
PEOPLE_LEADER.text: Here's what we know, what we don't, and what we're doing this week. Questions go here: [channel].

topics.title: 10-minute huddle script
topics.subtitle: A simple structure to make the next step obvious.

bridge_messages.INDIVIDUAL: Tell your leader what blocked you and what you tried.
bridge_messages.PEOPLE_LEADER: Protect capacity: one standard, one example, one practice.

role_specific_framing.INDIVIDUAL: Use this to ask for a clear weekly standard and practice focus.
role_specific_framing.PEOPLE_LEADER: Use this to create a predictable weekly rhythm during change.

INDIVIDUAL: Ask what the weekly standard is.
Ask what one example 'good' looks like.
Ask what to practice this week.
PEOPLE_LEADER: State the standard in one sentence.
Pick one practice task and one blocker question.

INDIVIDUAL.label: Ask for the weekly standard
INDIVIDUAL.text: What is the one thing we should do consistently this week in the new way?

PEOPLE_LEADER.label: 10-minute huddle
PEOPLE_LEADER.text: This week's expectation is: [one sentence]. Why it matters: [one sentence]. Today's change: [one sentence]. Practice: [5 minutes]. Blockers: [2 minutes].

topics.title: Ten-minute script builder
topics.subtitle: Build a structured leader script in five fields.

bridge_messages.INDIVIDUAL: If your leader hasn't communicated clearly yet, you can use this to show them what you need.
bridge_messages.PEOPLE_LEADER: Protect capacity: one standard, one example, one mistake to avoid, one practice, one blocker.

role_specific_framing.INDIVIDUAL: Use this to prepare for a conversation about what your team or manager needs to hear.
role_specific_framing.PEOPLE_LEADER: Use this to build a structured script before your next team message or huddle.

INDIVIDUAL: Identify what standard you need clarity on.
Describe what a good example looks like.
PEOPLE_LEADER: Share the output with your team.

topics.title: Stakeholder map
topics.subtitle: Map who matters, how much, and how to engage them.
topics.best_for: Planning stakeholder engagement, identifying champions and blockers

bridge_messages.INDIVIDUAL: Name the people who can unblock you — and how to approach them.
bridge_messages.PEOPLE_LEADER: Know who your champions, neutrals, and resistors are before the change hits hard.

role_specific_framing.INDIVIDUAL: Use this to identify who you need to work with — and how to approach each one.
role_specific_framing.PEOPLE_LEADER: Use this to prioritize your engagement. High influence + resistant = your biggest lever.

INDIVIDUAL: Add the people who affect your ability to change.

topics.title: Focus & influence sort
topics.subtitle: Sort your concerns into what you control, can influence, and need help with.
topics.best_for: Feeling overwhelmed, not knowing where to start

bridge_messages.INDIVIDUAL: You can't do everything. Name the one thing you'll focus on.
bridge_messages.PEOPLE_LEADER: Your team needs to see you remove barriers, not just set priorities.

role_specific_framing.INDIVIDUAL: Use this when you're overwhelmed and don't know where to start. Sort it, then act on just one thing.
role_specific_framing.PEOPLE_LEADER: Use this to separate what you can directly fix from what needs escalation or support.

INDIVIDUAL: Sort each into: I control / I can influence / I need help.
Pick one action and one ask.
PEOPLE_LEADER: Sort each by your actual control.
Remove one blocker visibly this week.

topics.title: Next step builder
topics.subtitle: One action. One check-in date. One success signal.
topics.best_for: Getting unstuck, translating insight into action

bridge_messages.INDIVIDUAL: A plan with a date is five times more likely to happen than one without.
bridge_messages.PEOPLE_LEADER: Make the next step obvious for your team — ambiguity is the enemy of momentum.

role_specific_framing.INDIVIDUAL: Use this when you know something needs to happen but it keeps not happening.
role_specific_framing.PEOPLE_LEADER: Use this to close any meeting or conversation with a clear, accountable next step.

PEOPLE_LEADER: Define the success signal you'll look for.

topics.title: Reaction profile
topics.subtitle: Understand your default change response — and what to do with it.

bridge_messages.INDIVIDUAL: Your reaction is data, not a verdict. Use it to decide your next move.
bridge_messages.PEOPLE_LEADER: Understanding how your team tends to react helps you meet them where they are.

role_specific_framing.INDIVIDUAL: Use this to understand your own patterns — not to judge yourself, but to choose your next move more intentionally.
role_specific_framing.PEOPLE_LEADER: Use this to understand your team's range of reactions and tailor your approach accordingly.

INDIVIDUAL: Choose one action from the recommendations.
PEOPLE_LEADER: Use the profile types to map your team.
Tailor your communication for each type.
Focus first on Resistors and Quit-risks.
